subject: English
style: Uganda flag-inspired classroom infographic; exact SVG text overlay; no generated text inside raster image.
title: School Holidays
details: Use 'am going to' for your own plans.
Ask: "Where are you going to go?"
evidence_excerpts: Listening comprehension focused on a story about Nakato’s plan to visit her grandmother up-country. The learner listens to a short narrative and identifies specific future plans mentioned. Listen to the story carefully.; Identify the characters.; List the places Nakato plans to visit.

Introduction of key terms: travel, plan, up-country, relatives, commence, and board. The learner uses holiday-related vocabulary correctly in sentences. Up-country: The village/rural area.; Commence: To start or begin.; Relatives: Family members like aunts and cousins.

Grammar practice focusing on "Subject + am/is/are + going to + verb." The learner constructs oral sentences using the "going to" structure for future plans. Use 'am going to' for your own plans.; Use 'is going to' for one person.; Use 'are going to' for many people.
